Technical Proposal: Engagement of an Agency/Organization for Setting up a Program Management Unit for Cluster 
Development Program of National Horticulture Board 
 Technical Proposal  69 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
CASE STUDY – SMART (State of Maharashtra’s Agri-business and Rural Transformation Program 
Project) 
One such project is SMART, under which GT has undertaken a few pilot sub projects and carried out activities 
under project interventions. Project intervention focuses on certain key areas –  
1. 
Supporting Enterprise Growth and Expanding Market Access - Enterprise development and 
technical support services, Market Access Plans, Productive Partnerships, Pilot program on Urban Food 
Systems, Access to finance, Enhancing inclusion of women 
2. 
Building Risk Mitigation Mechanisms - Enhanced market information and intelligence services, 
Strengthening the warehouse receipts systems, Improving the risk management capabilities of the State 
The project will support developing and partially financing Productive Partnerships (PP), between CBOs and 
markets, and Agri-SMEs and leading firms. Through this, project aims to develop a long-term, voluntary and 
commercial relationship that will help the partners to improve their competitiveness in terms of price, cost, 
productivity, quality, and sales volume and provides holistic solutions to address market imperfections that inhibit 
smallholder producers’ socio-economic progress.  
 
 
Objective of the Sub project –  
Facilitating development of long-term partnerships between CBOs and the private sector for development of 
value chains.  
Helping Community Based Organizations (CBOs) to engage in profitable market-oriented production and to 
provide improved access to markets through the provision of technology, information services, critical public 
infrastructure and linkages to agribusinesses. 
Creating and strengthening industry-wide partnerships along the value chain, thus forging linkages between 
CBOs, processors, and other stakeholders.  
Reducing existing obstacles to agriculture and food trade, thereby increasing the ability of farmers and 
agribusinesses to respond to traceability and food-quality standards to meet domestic and exports requirements.

Development Program of National Horticulture Board 
 Technical Proposal  70 
4.4 
Our Strategic Approach 
Theory of Change 
Our overarcing approach shall be to maximise sustained impact of the programme on societies in targeted 
geographies and communities. Based on international best practices, GT has developed an approach 
that establishes the logical causal relationships between the programme initiatives, outputs and impact 
using the theory of change (please refer the illustration). The theory of change underpins the robustness 
of programme design and checks for coherence, integration, risks and any overlaps in strategic approach.  
The programme goals in the TOR, specify the target groups and the improved performance expected. 
Improvements should ideally self-sustain, post the intervention project. In order to maximise sustained 
impact, our approach shall be to work with relevant stakeholders (levers) that already exists in or are 
available to the target group eco-systems. Collaboration by these stakeholders is achieved by alignment 
of goals and actions. The benefit is leverage of partner networks, social-capital resources, capacities and 
resources for mutual benefit. This not only improves the speed of progress and reduces costs, but 
simultaneously leads to greater adoption rates and therefore greater sustainability. The outputs/ 
deliverables specified in the TOR are carefully selected to function as catalysts of change which achieve 
the maximum impact. By constantly monitoring the most critical factors and engaging stakeholders, risks 
and issues are identified early and resolved promptly. 
We have developed a theory of change for the project based on our understanding, and the same is 
presented in below.

Our Adopted Methodology 
GT will prepare an outreach strategy as well as an implementation plan so that key activities, project 
enablers, actors, partners are identified to further carrying out implementation of project intervention 
components. Also, GT intends to put an effective monitoring and review mechanism till the completion of 
project. The outreach strategy will help in disseminate project’s objective to potential value chain players 
in the selected value chain. It will further help project to identify potential partners (CBOs, Processors, 
Institutional Buyers, Research Institutes, Architectural and Engineering Consultant etc.) for the 
development of model detailed project reports.  
Our solutions cater to the overall delivery of cluster development starting from concept to commissioning 
i.e. conducting gap studies, development of diagnostic reports & DPRs, marketing, design & 
development, capacity building, skill assessment, introduction of latest technologies, implementation 
support, monitoring & evaluation, assessment of skill development, business development services 
support, etc. Adopted methodology will include help and support to strengthen the backward & forward 
linkages for the small and marginal farmers with the help of productive partnership (PPs) that will establish 
a robust and efficient supply chain ensuring the quality of farm produce is at par with global standards to 
increase competitiveness in high end markets. It also aims to encourage farming community to adopt 
innovative farming technologies as well as mitigate risk through adoption of warehousing by getting into 
partnership with potential partners. Capacity development of farmers, private sector and Government 
officials would be an integral part of the program.

4.6 
Detailed Methodology 
Task 1: Inception report and resource mobilisation  
This includes literature review of available documents related to the project and mandate, Secondary 
Research on other ongoing projects in the state, consultation with NHB and preliminary visits to selected 
districts in the State. Visits are for interaction with a sample set of related stakeholder line departments, 
other service providers like KVK, Agriculture Universities, District Industries Centres (DICs), Lead Bank 
and potential beneficiaries, followed by preparation of detailed draft work plan and inception report and 
submission for review, incorporation of feedback and finalisation of inception report including Work Plan 
(with timelines, resources etc.).  
 
Task 2: Finalize the contours of Cluster Development Scheme, Operational Guidelines and related 
documentation. 
This service and deliverable under the assignment involves diagnostic study preparation, gap analysis, 
as well as value chain study of selected crops in identified clusters. Diagnostic study is to encompass 
cluster mapping and analysis of related stakeholders and validation. Notably, the study would also 
consider constraints in various critical factors and parameters including in factor conditions and other 
aspects of an enabling environment for business activities as well as demand conditions. This study will 
also provide pointers to support required from the Government/Government Agencies will drive the 
development of the Scheme for the Development of Clusters to ensure that Clusters such formed are 
vibrant, functional and commercially viable clusters. The Scheme thus developed, will outline all the 
required support - financial and otherwise from government and the relevant stakeholders as also broadly 
outline the roles and responsibilities of the key players. The scheme will be detailed through an 
operational guideline will be formulated which will include SoPs for each identified stakeholders. 
 
Diagnostic Study: Diagnostic study will involve both primary and secondary research, data compilation 
and analysis. A typical diagnostic study may comprise the following content structure: National and global 
scenario of selected crop value chain in the region, manufacturing process of commodity in the region as 
well as benchmarked process options, and an analysis of farm operations in different functional areas of 
agricultural practices (availability of input, procurement, finance, technology, infrastructure, market 
demand, business environment etc.). A judgemental stratified sample of stakeholders involved in different 
value chain activities will be selected for primary consultation. Also, related private and public 
stakeholders will be considered for study. A mix of personal interviews and FGDs will be deployed over 
consultation. The sample and parameters of study will be finalised in consultation with NHB. Validation 
will involve all stakeholders. Studies will be undertaken both in a broad sector context as well as followed 
by more detailed at the farm level. The typical scope and coverage of a diagnostic study report may be 
viewed as follows:

It is understood that a value chain basically comprises of activities involved in getting products from the 
producer to the consumer and focuses on the primary activities in the chain such as production, 
transportation, processing, and marketing and information exchange. Value chain analysis in a broad 
sense also encompasses the ‘rules of the game’ (i.e. the governance of the chain), as well as support 
services, such as quality certification. Agri value chains are typically “buyer driven” than “producer driven” 
chains. Therefore, analysis of value chains would require primary information through field survey. The 
major stakeholders, who may be covered for field study, are as follows:

Task 8: Ensure synergy and integration of cluster specific DPRs with ODOP (One District One 
Product) program of Ministry and newly launched scheme of FPOs. 
 
One District One Product (ODOP) was launched by the Government of Uttar Pradesh to encourage and 
revive aboriginals’ arts and craft products. The scheme would help Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises 
(MSME) to produce and promote products that are unique in Uttar Pradesh. Under this initiative, export 
of Bhadohi carpets has doubled from Rs 4,000 crore to Rs 8,000 crore. Taking inspiration from such 
success stories, GT will encourage PIAs to scout products which are specific to a particular cluster and 
can be positioned as a niche product. PIAs will develop DPRs involving growers, processors, FPOs for 
some specific product and avail benefits under ODOP (One District One Product) program. GT will also 
identify other schemes which are launched for newly formed FPOs to ensure the maximum impact as 
well as to pump more liquidity to the initiative.  
 
GT will also assist PIAs in dissemination of sub schemes under ODOP scheme to relevant stakeholders, 
which are –

Skill Development Scheme

Task 13: Create a real time Monitoring and Evaluation dashboard for streamlining process of 
implementation and undertake corrective action whenever required. 
 
In order for this overall objective to be successful it is imperative to adopt a robust Monitoring & Evaluation 
(M&E) plan. The purpose of the review or strategic evaluation is to provide an independent assessment 
of progress to date. This will test if the expected standards have been met in strategies and program 
design. The evaluation will support managerial decisions to make improvements in the next cycle of the 
program and simultaneously foster organizational and key stakeholder, insight and learning. The main 
purpose and objective of having a M&E plan in place is: -  
 
Verify project progress/ performance and inform decision makers reliably and insightfully 


An M&E approach supports development good practice and an M&E system serves multiple needs for 
information. The project priority need from M&E is to determine the merit of the intervention and viability 
for other projects. Interventions should be relevant to the context, coherent with other interventions, 
achieve their objectives, deliver results in an efficient way, and have a positive impact that is sustainable. 
The hypothesis for evaluation to verify is: -  
1. An approach to cluster value-chain, BDS and market-led development in India increasing the 
income of beneficiaries (in poor category) by an average of +40%, sustainably. 
2. The intervention is remarkably effective and efficient and is expected to generate the following 
impact: -   
– 
Enabling Partnerships for transfer of knowledge at the national, state and local level. 
Stakeholders would include NGOs, Industry Associations, Self Help

Technical Proposal: Engagement of an Agency/Organization for Setting up a Program Management Unit for Cluster 
Development Program of National Horticulture Board 
 Technical Proposal  88 
 
 
 
 
Case Study: GT’s experience in Monitoring & Evaluation 
Grant Thornton (GT) has been engaged with Ministry of Food Processing Industries for the past 9 years 
and assisting them in implementation of various projects. One of such projects is Mega Food Parks 
scheme. The projects have been complex and we need to identify one applicant out of multiples to 
implement a large infrastructure project, with a value of more than USD 20 million. The project envisages 
to support farmers more than 1000 in nos and shall have huge cluster transformation. GT has been 
appointed as review and monitoring agency. Considering the complexity, we have developed excel 
based tools to evaluate the applicants. We have also standardized the templates as there were multiple 
proposals from each state and 1 from each state is to be finalized. The tool has really worked out and 
support in easy evaluation of applications. Similar program with respect to project monitoring has also 
been designed.  
 
Team GT has developed a Dashboard for the MP government which provides a holistic 
view of the state, start from status and initiatives in health, labour, Industry, 
Agriculture, Horticulture and many more.

inclusion, Gender, Environment & Social Safeguards aspects. FPPs will be evaluated on following

GT with consultation with PIUs will plan field verification activity of submitted proposals. GT will record all 
the findings of field verification and a report will be prepared. Prepared record will be shared with NHB or 
Proposal Approval Committee as well as with PIAs so that they address any identified gaps in further 
DPR preparation. GT will also make recommendation in the prescribed format for release of financial 
assistance to those proposal which are meeting the eligibility criteria.

specific clusters 
 
GT will assist in monitoring and supervision of ongoing project implementation. GT will design an MIS 
system to track progress of the project activities. The developed MIS system will not only keep track of 
the progress but will also help in talking corrective measures in the nick of the time thus mitigating the 
risk.

a. Designing: MIS will be developed based on consultation with all concerned stakeholders and 
PIAs of the project. Firstly, a report will be prepared describing the system and methodology to 
be adopted, roles and responsibilities of the implementing staff/agencies that will be involved in 
monitoring and IT systems that need be acquired. System will capture data of members, 
meetings conducted, training programmes conducted, production at FPC as well as enterprise 
level, marketable surplus available, transactions completed etc. 
b. Implementation: Data is to be collected on real time basis and reports be generated on regular 
basis (fortnightly, monthly etc). Standardisation of data is very critical part to secure best 
analysis. GT has developed templates for standardization and can be customized based on the 
specific requirements. 
c. Review: Based on the reports, results will be evaluated. Results will be compared with the 
implementation schedules and yearly activity plan and gaps will be identified 
d. Resolving measures: Identification of gaps, after discussion with PIAs, will be communicated 
to respective teams so that corrective measures can be taken immediately and deviation from 
the target can be brought back to standard course.

Technical Proposal: Engagement of an Agency/Organization for Setting up a Program Management Unit for Cluster 
Development Program of National Horticulture Board 
 Technical Proposal  91 
Task 16. Agency will also work on new concepts and strategies with NHB for development of 
horticulture sector (like Knowledge Accelerator, Lead Firm linkages, New Farm Technologies, 
Integration with Startups, Internet of Things (lOT), Block Chain.  
 
This service will involve compilation of equipment and process technologies from public and private sector 
service providers including (machinery and equipment manufacturers, R & D institutions including from 
Rural Research Laboratories, CFTRI (Mysore), IIPHT etc. This will be followed by short-listing new and 
innovative technologies and preparation of a “technology benchmarking document” on existing regional 
versus benchmarked technology. Several appropriate new and innovative technologies such as portable 
cold storages, reverse milling machines, closed goat farming, solar vegetable drying equipment and solar 
based reefer vans have been introduced. Advanced technology IQF and blast freezing technologies have 
also been widely disseminated by GT in similar assignments. GT will develop Knowledge Accelerator, 
which will undertake capacity building of farmers on Good Agricultural Practices (especially since FSSAI 
has come up with MRL limits for field crops and horticultural Crops); create and disseminate extension 
material for supporting diversification of horticulture production through technology and knowledge 
transfer, including harvest, delivery and distribution of water; assessment, collation and dissemination of 
quality and compliance parameters of various export markets; assessment of price trends and competition 
analysis of various markets; assessment of existing post-harvest infrastructure including cold stress and 
markets and facilities therein including technology and capacity utilization and Geo-tag these facilities; 
develop package of practices for farmers to adopt farm practices as per  export market requirements. GT 
will also work on software based technologies such as IoT and Blockchain and establish a digital 
information portal which will serve as a platform for real-time consultation between stakeholders, facilitate 
early warning of SPS and TBT changes and barriers, collate global best practices on promoting horti 
value chains.  
 
 
 
 
Case Study: GT’s experience in Blockchain and IOT 
GT has developed and implemented blockchain for Aditya Birla Group to augment 
and optimize their business process

 Technical Proposal  96 
Transfer of Knowledge  
Grant Thornton has a proprietary knowledge sharing and collaboration platform, which could 
be utilised for capturing and sharing knowledge amongst the stakeholders. The proposed State 
Level Team, Subject Matter Experts and identified mentors would be the specialists who will 
catalyse and drive the knowledge transfer and management plan. Knowledge transfer would 
be systematically captured throughout the programme. The tools will enhance the reach of the 
experts, interventions, number of beneficiaries and facilitate transfer of learning. The 
beneficiaries of the assignment shall be trained on the proprietary tools.   
 
 
Grant Thornton proposes following key actions for creating a robust knowledge base 
and processes for dissemination: 
 
 
Separate need based training modules which are IT based for beneficiaries. Various blogs, 
WhatsApp groups, would be created for effective communication among all stakeholders. 
 
All stakeholders will have access to the knowhow and learnings provided by the experts 
 
Create theme-based focus groups for innovation and problem solving across locations, 
which would be focused of the requirement of target entrepreneurs 
 
Create a knowledge repository on the platform for free access by stakeholders and 
knowledge transfer 
 
Experts will also provide hyperlinks to the best available sources of technology information 
 
The experts will utilise blogs to keep stakeholders informed about developments and 
events 
 
Discussion forums on the platform will enable dialogue on key themes and topics 
 
Learning is captured and instantly shared with other FPOs/Entrepreneurs for replication 
 
Issues encountered will also realise immediate attention from the technical team 
 
This type of knowledge sharing and network formation will also be utilised to integrate and 
engage with other stakeholders/ organisations related to the project.

Name of assignment or project: conducting an Impact 
Evaluation Study of Schemes of NHB 
Year: January 2020-July 2020 
Location: Pan India 
Client: National Horticulture Board, Ministry of Agriculture & 
Farmers Welfare 
Position Held: Client Services Partner 
Main Features: NHB intends to have this Impact Evaluation 
Study for the purpose of assessing its effectiveness in meeting 
the objectives for which the schemes were conceptualization. 
The result of the evaluation would be used as inputs for further 
modifications/ improvement in the ongoing schemes of 
NHB.    The services of a suitable “Bidder” (Agencies 
Organization) is required to be outsourced as per the provisions 
contained in “Manual of Policies and Procedure of Employment 
of Consultant” (GFR Rules) issued by Department of 
Expenditure, Ministry of Finance, Government of India read with 
relevant instructions issued from time to time by inviting Request 
for Proposal (RFP) from reputed & qualified “Bidders” for carrying 
out Impact Evaluation Study.  To determine the relevance and 
fulfilment of aims, as well as efficacy and effectiveness of 
implementing the scheme.  The objectives of the scheme: 
 
Evaluate the impact of the scheme in terms of increase in 
area, production and productivity of various horticulture crops 
covered under NHB. 
 
Evaluate the infrastructure created for INM/IPM, post-harvest 
management and marketing. 
 
Find out whether the states are adopting cluster approach. 
 
The extent of farmer’s satisfaction. 
 
Analyze of the effectiveness of publicity, mass media and 
information technology awareness of NHB. 
 
Find out the actual deliverables – support services, record 
keeping, institutional capacity building, reporting and 
monitoring of the schemes.  
 
Study 
the 
convergence 
and 
synergy 
with 
ongoing 
programmes like NREGS, Micro Irrigation, APEDA, MoFPI, 
NCDC, DMI and SFAC etc. 
 
Analyze of the effectiveness of publicity, mass media and 
information technology awareness of NHB. 
 
Initiate necessary action for variations and deviations in the 
field. 
 
Bench mark the beast practices for reference and adoption. 
 
Assess the overall impact of the programmes implemented 
under NHB with state participation. 
Activities Performed: 
 
To examine the structure of various NHB schemes, cost 
norms, pattern & quantum of financial assistance and 
comprehensiveness of the guidelines of these schemes and 
suggest improvements. 
 
To study impact of NHB schemes on attracting private and 
institutional investments, employment generation, enhancing 
living standards of work force and on exports, and suggest

Name of assignment/job or project: Development and 
Implementation of Integrated Silo Complexes on Hub and 
Spoke Model of 300,000 MT 
Year: November 2013 – July 2015  
Location: Bihar, Uttar Pradesh 
Client: Private Investors 
Main project features: Food grain storage is a major 
necessity in the backward states, particularly w.r.t to meeting 
the food security need and incentivizing agricultural 
production. The hub and spoke model primarily is a departure 
from the erstwhile mechanism of creating large storage 
facilities at select locations. Also the pressure on land coupled 
with issues related to due diligence and time delays in 
procuring large land parcels along with issues related to grain 
inventory management has resulted in paradigm shift towards 
creating small vertical steel silos complexes with value adding 
facility like quality linked payment mechanism, cleaning, 
drying, bagging etc.

Activities performed:

Overall Development and Implementation of Integrated 
Silo complexes on Hub and Spoke Model of a capacity of 
300,000 MT

establishment of Spices Park and understand the operational 
dynamics and suitability of the region to cater to the 
requirements of different processing units within the proposed 
Spices Park and also whether the investments justify the 
returns. Feasibility report included Detailed study on supply

quality control infrastructure, that needs to be created along 
with block costs, identification of potential investors, 
development of institutional framework etc.

Name of assignment/job or project: Feasibility study for 
Identification of locations for Mega Food Parks in Five 
Zones of India  
Year: 2007-08 
Location: All Over India 
Client: Ministry of Food Processing Industries, Government of 
India 
Main project features: The Ministry had proposed the launch 
the Mega Food Park Scheme during the Eleventh Five Year 
Plan with an aim to assist the development of adequate / 
excellent infrastructure facilities for food processing along the 
value chain from the farm to market – including the creation of 
infrastructure near the farm, transportation, logistics and 
centralised processing centers. The main feature of the 
scheme is a cluster based approach. The objective of this 
assignment was aimed at assessing the viability of the Mega 
Food Parks in identified clusters with respect to an outcome of 
increased realization for farmers, creation of high quality rural 
processing infrastructure, reduction in wastage, capacity 
building of the producers and processors and creation of an 
efficient supply chain along with significant direct and indirect 
employment generation 
Positions held: Agri Supply Chain Expert  
Activities performed:

Assessing the viability of the Mega Food Parks in identified 
clusters with respect to an outcome of increased 
realization for farmers, creation of high quality rural 
processing infrastructure, reduction in wastage, capacity 
building of the producers and processors and creation of 
an efficient supply chain along with significant direct and 
indirect employment generation

food programs in these countries

Technical Proposal: Engagement of an Agency/Organization for Setting up a Program Management Unit for Cluster Development Program of National Horticulture Board 
 Technical Proposal  195 
3.  Post-harvest 
Technology Expert (1 
No.) 
Deepti Thakur 
12 
i. 
Capacity building and export oriented training & skill development for 
entrepreneurs, producers, processor and exporters. 
ii. 
Capacity building of Farmers and Farmer Producer Organization 
(FPOs) on PHM practices including ripening, packaging and transport 
of produce commensurate to export requirements. 
iii. 
Infrastructure gap analysis by assessment of existing Post-Harvest 
Infrastructure and facilities including technology and capacity 
utilization for holistic development of identified clusters. 
iv. 
Facilitate linkages with domestic markets by leveraging economies of 
scale for quality and price efficiency in transportation, storage, 
marketing and distribution of horticulture produce. 
v. 
Facilitate linkages with export markets including assessment and 
collation of quality and compliance parameters of various targeted 
export markets, advocacy for regarding various Free Trade 
Agreements (FTAs), trade policies and developing compliance 
ecosystem in identified clusters with respect to region specific 
Technical Barriers to Trade (TBTs) and Sanitary or Phytosanitary 
(SPS) requirements 
vi. 
Ensure synergized interventions by State Agriculture/ Horticulture 
Department, Food Processing Departments and Central Government 
Departments/Ministries in identified clusters. 
vii. 
Promote value addition and export of organic products, registration of
